records.ety: [AS. rendan, hrendan; cf. OFries. renda, randa, Fries. renne to cut, rend, Icel. hrinda to push, thrust, AS. hrindan; or cf. Icel. ræna to rob, plunder, Ir. rannaim to divide, share, part, W. rhanu, Armor. ranna.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Rent (rnt); p. pr. & vb. n. Rending.]

defs: To separate into parts with force or sudden violence; to tear asunder; to split; to burst; as, powder rends a rock in blasting; lightning rends an oak.
quotes: The dreadful thunder Doth rend the region.

defs: To part or tear off forcibly; to take away by force.
quotes: An empire from its old foundations rent.
I will surely rend the kingdom from thee.

defs: To be rent or torn; to become parted; to separate; to split.
